grigson | Chart Format | CLI Reference | Renderer | Testing | Demo

grigson

A plain-text format and toolkit for writing chord charts.

Grigson is named after Lionel Grigson, author of The Jazz Chord Book. Source files use the .chart extension and look like the charts they describe — pipe characters for bar lines, standard chord names, YAML front matter for metadata.

---
title: "Blues in F"
key: F
feel: "swing"
---

[Head]
||: (4/4) F7 | % | % | % |
| Bb7 | % | F7 | % |
| C7 | Bb7 | F7 | C7 :||

Documentation

  • Chart Format — the .chart file format reference
  • CLI Reference — the grigson command-line tool
  • Renderer — plain-text and SVG renderers, configuration, and custom renderers
  • Testing — test strategy, conventions, and coverage

grigson — a .chart file parser and normaliser
